subject: Re: tensorflow error when particle picking
body:
The picking model is in the right path. A model can only be selected in the 
Warp GUI if it is in the correct directory (you can't choose the directory 
where it searches for picking models).

The fact Warp knows the name of the picking model (in the error message) 
prooves that it is in the correct directory.

Maybe a permissions problem since I use a session that doesn't have admin 
privileges, but I took care to install Warp in a directory that is 
accessible without admin privileges (I saw that advice somewhere in the 
documentation). But I'll check on that and let you know. Thanks for your 
inputs.

Best,

Olivier

On Wednesday, 5 January 2022 at 13:40:03 UTC+1 Pranav Shah wrote:

> As error messages go, it is quite self-explanatory. Have you tried
> storing the picking model in the path specified in the error message?
> Best,
> Pranav
> --
> Pranav Shah
> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
>
> Division of Structural Biology,
> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
> University of Oxford,
> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
> UK
>
> On Wed, Jan 5, 2022 at 12:28 PM Olivier Le Bihan
> <olivier....@outlook.com> wrote:
> >
> > Hi Pranav,
> >
> > Thank you for your feedback. Yes I have seen that post but users mainly 
> complain about very slow particles picking, my problem is more severe since 
> I can't pick at all.
> >
> > The error message I got is the exact same as Dominique's error (see 
> Dominique's screenshot of error message in the first message of this 
> thread). The error message is pointing to a problem with TensorFlow but it 
> could be a silly mistake from my side since it indicates a problem reading 
> the model.
> >
> > As more and more users will switch to recent GPU hardware, this problem 
> is going to be a major limitation for Warp's usability by the community I 
> suppose. The RTX 3090 launched in september 2020, it is not particularly 
> new anymore and my hope is that Warp will ship with TensorFlow 2.0 in the 
> near future. That being said, I really like Warp otherwise and I can 
> totally understand the amount of work that would be needed to adapt the 
> code for TF 2.0...
> >
> > So I am wondering if the source of my problem (picking not working at 
> all and rising an error message) is the same as others problem (slow 
> picking) or are these problems unrelated.
> >
> > Best,
> >
> > Olivier
> >
> >
> >
> > On Wednesday, 5 January 2022 at 11:01:01 UTC+1 Pranav Shah wrote:
> >>
> >> Hi,
> >> There is an incompatibility between the latest Nvidia hardware and the
> >> TF that Warp ships with; see this thread
> >> https://groups.google.com/g/warp-em/c/Gryyk4cHp2M
> >>
> >> Best,
> >> Pranav
> >> --
> >> Pranav Shah
> >> Postdoctoral Research Fellow.
> >>
> >> Division of Structural Biology,
> >> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics,
> >> University of Oxford,
> >> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN,
> >> UK
> >>
> >> On Wed, Jan 5, 2022 at 9:15 AM Olivier Le Bihan
> >> <olivier....@outlook.com> wrote:
> >> >
> >> > Hi there,
> >> >
> >> > Let me rephrase my question; I would be interested to know if anyone 
> in here ever succeeded in making an RTX 3000 series GPU to work with the 
> BoxNet particle picking tool of Warp and what Nvidia driver / Cuda version 
> combinaison they used?
> >> >
> >> > After a bit of googling, I found out many people have problems with 
> RTX3090 / Cuda 11 and TensorFlow 1.XX. Since Warp is using TensorFlow 1.1, 
> I thought maybe that is the root cause of my issues.
> >> >
> >> > Could anyone please comment on that?
> >> >
> >> > Best,
> >> >
> >> > Olivier
> >> >
> >> >
> >> > On Thursday, 2 December 2021 at 14:15:30 UTC+1 Olivier Le Bihan wrote:
> >> >>
> >> >> Hi all,
> >> >>
> >> >> I have the same problem with the exact same error message running 
> Warp 1.09 on a windows 10 threadripper machine with an RTX 3090 and 128 Gb 
> of RAM.
> >> >>
> >> >> Processing works fine otherwise except for the particle picking tool.
> >> >>
> >> >> Would the Warp nightly build 2020-11-04 have any chance of fixing 
> the problem?
> >> >>
> >> >> Regards,
> >> >>
> >> >> Olivier Le Bihan
> >> >>
> >> >> On Wednesday, 1 December 2021 at 21:07:16 UTC+1 Dominique wrote:
> >> >>>
> >> >>> Hello I have a windows laptop with these specs
> >> >>>
> >> >>> Processor Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-9750H CPU @ 2.60GHz 2.60 GHz
> >> >>> Installed RAM 32.0 GB (31.8 GB usable)
> >> >>> Device ID C7DFC50F-AE3A-4D98-8B8C-0790472B6BEF
> >> >>> Product ID 00325-81709-59805-AAOEM
> >> >>> System type 64-bit operating system, x64-based processor
> >> >>> 1X Nvidia GeForce GTX 1660 Ti
> >> >>>
> >> >>> I installed WARP 1.09 on it and everything ran except for particle 
> picking. I get the error message in the screenshot.
